name: Albany County Pickleball
description: Port-a-potty available. Courts adjacent to Albany County Hockey.

name: Capital District YMCA- Bethlehem Branch
description: Capital District YMCA- Bethlehem Branch is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Delmar, NY. There are 3 indoor wood courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. Amenities include locker rooms, restrooms, and water. A membership is required to play.

name: Colonie Mohawk River Park
description: No charge for Town of Colonie Residents to enter gate to park if they have a fob obtained from the Town. Non-residents must pay $10.30. Most available times for open courts are between 12:30 - 4:30. In addition, courts can be reserved by paying an hourly rental fee. If not, first come first play.
hours: Opens daily 10:30a.m. - 7:00p.m. May 23, 24, 25, May 30, 31 and June 6, 7, and June 13 thru September 6, 2026 (weather permitting) Note to Town of Colonie Residents: You must obtain a Resident FOB to enter the Colonie Town Pool for the 2026 season. No exceptions. Please visit the Parks and Recreation Office from 8:00 a.m. until 3:45 p.m. at 71 Schermerhorn Road, Cohoes, NY 12047.

name: Latham Kiwanis Park
description: Latham Kiwanis Park is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Latham, NY. There are 2 outdoor hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. Players have access to restrooms and water. The courts are free.

name: Milton Pocket Park
description: Come play pickleball at Milton Pocket Park in Albany, NY! There are 4 outdoor hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The courts are free.

name: Palma Park
description: Come play pickleball at Palma Park in Albany, NY! There are 2 outdoor hard courts. The lines are overlaid on a tennis court, so the net is a little taller than a normal pickleball net. The courts are free.

name: Picklers Pickleball Club
description: Picklers Pickleball Club is a place to play pickleball in Menands, NY. There are 6 indoor hard, concrete, and acrylic courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. Reservations are available. The facility includes amenities like food, locker rooms, restrooms, water, lights, and trainers or lessons. A one-time fee is required to play.
fees: Fee required — $12 for 2-hours open play or $48/hour for reserving an indoor court.

name: Rudy A. Ciccotti Family Rec Center
description: Come play pickleball at Rudy A. Ciccotti Family Rec Center in Albany, NY! There are 4 indoor courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. A membership is required to play. Players have access to locker rooms, restrooms, water, and wheelchair accessibility.

name: St. Vincents PAL Gym
description: St. Vincents PAL Gym is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Albany, NY. There are 2 indoor wood courts. The lines are permanent, but you'll need to bring your own net. Amenities include restrooms. A membership is required to play.
hours: - Monday-Friday -- 9:00 am-3:00 pm Login powered by eCatholic ®

name: Tri-City Fitness
description: Tri-City Fitness is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Latham, NY.

name: True Pickleball Club
description: Come play pickleball at True Pickleball Club in Latham, NY! There are 6 indoor hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. A one-time fee is required to play. Amenities include food, water, restrooms, trainers or lessons, a pro shop or equipment store, and lights. The courts can be reserved.
